codeReview.summary: The feature adds coverage for polyline families and hatch document visibility, but the bridge still drops semantics required by the feature contract. In particular, polyface/polygon mesh structure is collapsed to a flat vertex list and hatch conversion hard-panics on valid spline boundary paths.

issues.description: `PolygonMesh` and `PolyfaceMesh` are converted to native `EntityData::Polyline` by copying only vertex positions (and, for polyface, width/bulge fields). acadrust meshes also carry mesh dimensions / face records (`vendor_tmp/acadrust/src/entities/polygon_mesh.rs`, `vendor_tmp/acadrust/src/entities/polyface_mesh.rs`), but h7cad-native-model has no fields for them and the bridge silently discards them on compat->native conversion. This violates the feature requirement to preserve family type and vertex data for classic polyline families, because a bridged polyface mesh cannot round-trip its boundary/face structure and a polygon mesh loses its M/N mesh shape metadata.

`acad_hatch_path_to_native()` panics on `BoundaryEdge::Spline(_)`. acadrust HATCH boundary paths explicitly support spline edges (`vendor_tmp/acadrust/src/entities/hatch.rs`), so any compat document containing a spline hatch loop will crash during bridge-back instead of preserving boundary structure or failing gracefully. The feature contract explicitly calls for preserving hatch boundary structure through native/compat/native conversion, so this hard panic leaves valid HATCH content unsupported.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `dxf-bridge-worker` skill tells workers to run `cargo test --lib native_bridge`, but this workspace has no root library target; the worker transcript shows that command failing before switching to the working binary test filter `cargo test --bin H7CAD native_bridge -- --test-threads=16`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `D:/work/plant-code/cad/H7CAD/.factory/skills/dxf-bridge-worker/SKILL.md` step 8 vs. worker transcript skeleton for session `79b1291b-b0ac-4e4e-92ac-d096142409a8`, which records `cargo test --lib native_bridge -- --test-threads=16` failing with `error: no library targets found in package `H7CAD``.

summary: Review failed. The bridge no longer drops the targeted entities entirely, but it still does not preserve polyface/polygon mesh semantics and it can panic on spline hatch boundaries, so the implementation does not fully satisfy the feature requirements.
